ead.archdesc:
======= RMA03306Cornell University Library Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections exhibition materials13-6-3306Compiled by Julia Parker, 10/13/2003Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
October 13, 20032B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:

English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections exhibition materials
Cornell University. Library. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
13-6-3306
10 cubic feet. 10 cubic feet.
1992-2018 Pamphlets, posters, note cards Division exhibit items, records, and related material. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 23 701 699 700 802-804 ======= 23 701 699 700 802-804 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 4859569
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Julia Parker
Date completed:
October 13, 2003
EAD encoding:
Julia Parker, October 13, 2003
Date modified
RMC Staff, September 2018
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
The Division was formed in 1992 with the combination of the Department of Rare Books, the Department of Manuscripts and University Archives, the History of Science Collections, and the Icelandic Collection.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Division exhibit items, records, and related material.
Also DVDs of Reunion and other exhibition-related lectures including "History of Wine," by Thomas Pinney, June 5, 2008; "The Collector and the Editor: Tales from the Mark Twain Papers," Robert H. Hirst, April 23, 2010; and "All Full of Tears and Flapdoodle: The Genius of Mark Twain," June 10, 2010.
Copy of exhibition guest book and visitor questionnaires, 2008-2009.
SERIES LIST
Series I. General Library Files
Series II. Exhibits: Undated, 1951-1990
Series III. Exhibits: 1991-2000
Series IV. Exhibits: 2001-2016
Cite As:
Cornell University Library Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections exhibition materials, 1992-2012 #13-6-3306. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Libraries -- New York (State) -- Special collections. Library exhibits. Cornell University -- : Libraries.

Scope and Contents
Cooper Collection, 1990; Will that building stay up?, 1990; Andrew D. White and the History of Science, 1965 & 1982; Christmas Rose, 1983-4; parrots, 1984; Lavoisier (Grolier exhibit cards); Adelmann; acquisitions, new, 1974; acquisitions, recent, 1975; "An Affair of the Heart," 1983; bindings, 1981; "The Blue Jay and Its Illustrators," 1983; calculating instruments & books containing calculating devices, 1980; "coaching and carriage design," 1974; Elliot - Monograph of the Pittidae, 1983; facsimiles of famous works, 1976, 400th anniversary of Wm. Harvey's birth, 1978; "Health of Scholars," 1982; horse books, 1974; Robert Jameson, 1974; Marcello Malpighi's 350th birthday anniversary, 1978; oil resources, 1974; Joseph Prestele, 1982; "Reckoning with Figures"; Dorothy Schullian, 1989; "A Season for Remembering," 1980, 1982 envelopes)History of Science materials (letter size envelopes of cards)10
Scope and Contents
Academia del Cimento, 1966; Aranzio and embryology, 1964; Laura Bassi, Physicist, 1966; Claude Bernard, 1978; the Bunsen burner, 1964; "Monuments in Clinical Medicine," 1964; Sir Humphry Davy, 1965; "Early Accounts of the Moon," 1965; Fabricius, teacher of Harvey, 1963; famous dissertations in embryology and anatomy, 1963; Goethe and color, 1966; Harvey, Dr. William, 1963; recent acquisitions in the history of geology, 1964; history of science collections, 1964; first editions of Johann Kepler, 1964; Lavoisier; books from Lavoisier's library; Leeuwenhoek and the microscope; Mendel's experiments, 1965; "Multum In Parvo," 1962; "Not recorded in NUC," 1963; printing of science and the mind of man, 1963; books in the History of Science, 1966; Ready Reckoners, 1964; Rösel von Rosenhof's insects, 1965; Mauro Rusconi, 1965; Seba's cabinet of natural history, 1964; Niels Stensen, 1964; Emanuel Swedenborg, 1964; Trilussa's dental caricatures, 1965; Andreas Vesalius, Anatomist, 1964; William Whewell, 1966History of Science exhibition materials (small envelopes of cards)10
Scope and Contents
Jacques Gautier D'Agoty, 1970; anthropology exhibit, 1976; Petrus Apianus, 1967; apples, 1966; Audubon, John James; The Bent and Broken Moon, 1969; Thomas Bewick, 1978; bindings by Ivan Ruzicka, 1973; bleaching and dyeing, 1968; Boncompagni's birthday, 1978; the Boyle collection, 1981; watercolors from Brunfels' herbal, 1977; A Salute to George Lincoln Burr, 1972; Cannabis sativa, 1970; Christmas Rose and Star of Bethlehem, 1975; the Clepsydra, 1969; Curtis' Botanical Magazine, 1982; Henry Pelouze de Forest, Cornellian 1971; early mining, 1967; Charles Estienne as anatomist, 1967; fabulous animals, 1969; facsimile editions, 1968; five Jesuit scientists, 1968; the Jay Gould cable, 1966; Albrecht von Haller, 1977; the Hollister collection, 1967; Ithaca imprints, 1968; "It's Raining Violets," 1971; "Je Le Pansay; Diew Le Guarit," 1967; treasures of Mann Library, 1977; Meleagris gallopavo, 1968; medical dissertations, 1970; music in science, 1968; natural history museums, 1972; Ornithogallum umbellatum; 1969; Eliot Porter, the seasons, 1970; the quetzal, 1971; Rédouté: liliacées, 1970; roses in June, 1970 & 1982; scientific incunabula, 1969; scientific medals, 1969 (?); "Self-Poised upon that Yellow Flower," 1967; silkworms, 1969; suspension bridges, 1970; tea, 1969; tides, 1968; travel books of science, 1968; imitators of Vesalius, 1967; Vesuvius, 1969; Viscum album, 1969-1970; Andrew D. White and the History of Science, 1965; wine making, 1971; exhibit for College of Agriculture alumni, 1977; exhibit for Will Provine's History of Science classes, 1974; exhibit for S.L.A. Upstate N.Y. meeting, 1974; L. P. Williams History of Science class; 1976; recent acquisitions: December 1972; January-September, November-December 1973; March-June, September-October, December 1974; February-December 1975; January-December 1976; January-November 1977; January, June-October 1978History of Science exhibition materials: box of small miscellaneous cards10Autograph exhibit labels1963131Clothing: A Reflection of Cultural Changes1966132Clothing: A Reflection of Cultural Change poster1966914Shaw at Cornell: The Burgunder Collection1975133Reunion1975134Tompkins County exhibit cards1976135Political Americana exhibition materials1976-1988136Lavoisier files19791983-19841994137Department reading room exhibition case1980s138Political Campaign Collections, Nancy Dean's folder1980139Ephemera exhibit1980140Javanese Postcards file1981141Cornell Library exhibition1982142-44Alumni exhibit in Barton Hall1982145Nancy Dean exhibit ideas1982-1991146Reunion notes1983147Presidential exhibit1984148Presidential Campaign198421Political Americana198422Shaw files and labels198523-440th Anniversary198525Urban America: Documenting the Planners198526-15
